title: goeppertia clivorum
intro: Goeppertia clivorum, also known as Calathea lancifolia or 'Rattlesnake Plant', is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, characterized by its striking patterned leaves that resemble a snake's skin. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its roots and leaves. The health benefits of Goeppertia clivorum include the potential treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety in modern medicine.
